_note: TEMPLATE. Copy to config/devices.local.json (gitignored) and describe YOUR fleet. This is the multi-device registry (#485) the monitor-all view (#486) reads to attribute N devices' streams to the plants. IDENTITY (ADR-0027, Accepted): 'device_id' is the board's STABLE MINTED ID — a 6-char Crockford base32 nonce the firmware mints at first boot and emits on the wire at schema_version>=3 (#601/#618). It is the registry KEY and never changes on rename. The registry treats it as opaque (it does not validate the format). 'name'/'hostname' (#592) are the MUTABLE LABELS over it — renaming edits the label, never the key. #713 PLANT-FIRST: the UI leads with the PLANT, then the physical SENSOR (your sN sticker), then the BOARD by its friendly 'name' + physical 'side' (left/right on the ledge) — device_id never shows as a human label. 'side' is optional (left/right/etc), absent-safe. CHANNELS: the token (s1..s4) is the board PORT; 'probe' is the physical sensor sticker (s1..s12) YOU labeled and plugged into that port (ADR-0027 §5 splits Channel from Probe — sN in the UI is always your probe, never the port index); 'plant_id'/'plant_name' is the plant on that channel, with optional 'plant_type' + 'pot_size' plant-first enrichment (absent-safe). Order in this array is the dashboard's first-seen card order (#583). Optional 'base_url' (served root, e.g. http://192.168.1.42): live-polled when set — omit for tethered/serial-only (the example ships none so a fresh checkout never blocks polling a placeholder). Optional 'previous_ids' (#602): ids this board reported under before its current key; coalesced into one card/file lineage at display time, raw records untouched; an id equal to another device's live device_id is ignored (a live identity is never swallowed). Consumers depend on device_registry.py's functions, not this file's layout. W1 scope (#619): labels only — no assignment table / Site / moves (those are ADR-0027 W2+). Placeholder values below.
